column9: This lesson was for TARA-NADO. It was challenging for a few reasons. The lessons here are always outside so our materials constantly fly all over the place. Particularly, the styrofoam cup does not work for this lesson. It breaks under the pressure of the balloon. The pom poms were flying around and the cups were too. Overall a challenging lesson on its own made even more so by the location of the class. It might also be helpful to get notified about fire drills. Kim had to rush the lesson because the fire drill cut into our class time.

column9:
Children seemed to waltz in and out to talk to their parents. Dave was not responding to the movement and his back was turned from the door. I hoped he would be alerted by this. I walked outside to confirm that the child was talking to his parent and waited till the student was seated again.

A handful of kids decided to go to the restroom and this made things rowdy. One of the parents came inside and followed us to the restroom. Dave continued his lesson and seemed to talk over the rising raucous. I also noticed that the children were struggling with the styrofoam cup so I went round fixing it. Overall Dave was more focused on getting through the lesson and missed a lot of the other needs of a classroom. I do think he can be a better professor; he just needs to be more aware of his surroundings and respond accordingly.

asked staff to try and do the project prior to the students arrival to the classroom to see if any unexpected issues come up while running the class. had a student who pushed back the entire class (robert).

first time meeting staff. check in was easy. the classroom provided was very nice and open where she could spread out. some students struggled with supplies. some students noticed that the lighbulb was getting really hot.

first observe, check in was quick scan ID in front office. there is usually parking in front of the school. staff used the expectations i recommended the first day and it really helped. staff had a great rapport with students and they loved her fun demeanor.

parking is right in front of the school and easy check in. class room is a art room suggest putting down paper plate if using chemicals. also suggested using the white board (always use all of your space.

regular check in you can take a staff parking or on the street arrive early to avoid carpool

staff asked me to help out b/c she was feeling under the weather. check in was easy students are brought to the classroom for us. Ms. G is their regular school teacher and assists in out class, she also translates for the students who dont speak english.
normal check in filled isabel in with what i already know about the school from past observes. class went off with out a hitch students really liked staff and activity.

check in is easy. class takes place in the bungalows room 9. jenny took my suggestion ans sat the kids closer to her. always recommend to use the white board for the "3 be's" (be safe, be respectful, be responsible.) and to put the name of the activity.
check in is quick just let the security where you're going. there were 3 students that needed more of a 1 on 1. this class would definitely benefit from having a seating chart.
The staff is responsible for taking roll.
location was interesting really liked the scenery two different types of benches to work with but having the tray really helps.
it was really cold remind students to bring a sweater its a windy park
the students are little would benefit from prepping really well and proportioning out the chemicals to keep the class going class ended late.
check is the same the class did get smaller

the class is mixed grade i always suggest mixing the grades so the older students can help the younger ones. we had an incident with a student that was over stimulated and had an episode on class. the student will no longer be joining the class. there is a teacher in the classroom to assist with the other students.

check in was easy. class was very engaging. was walked to the classroom had no issue finding it students are brought to the class. for pick up we drop them off at the auditorium.
check in and drop off is the same as the day prior. pick up we are asked to drop the m off at the auditorium.
check in was quick we had two classes going the same day. this class was in room 24.

Campus has a quick check in and staff walked me to the classroom. Staff would benefit from a stop watch. Large class prep is necessary.
Check in was easy park in the parking lot. Staff was very prepared and had an excellent set up. Classroom is large so students can spread out and use the whole classroom
Check in was regular as usual. Staff was prepared with preset supplies struggles when needed to wash bottles for last part of the project.
check was regular. street parking is usually available and close. staff didnt have the best day  and was very irritable, but was able to get through the activity
parking is a little rough so go extra early. staff walks you to the classroom. staff had a 180 from last observe. very flexible and patient.

entrance is on kittridge. it is a secondary school so has a seprate entrance. staff walked us to check in with ID scan and printed badge. class was small, school staff said they expected it bc it was the first day back from spring break. assistant helped with assembly and supply (staff will always have an assistant in this class).

i was running late but got to class 10 min late. street parking is available and its recommended you get there extra early if you want close parking. security will let you in and campus staff will walk you to the class room. class is small so its easily managed. there is a student with a lot of energy but staff was able to redirect and encourage creativity
THIS IS FOR AGBU! she did great for her first lesson she needs a little but of practice but has great potential.

school has a easy check in and has underground parking use intercom to get in. you can ask a custodian to open the exit. class is held in the auditorium, custodians set up tables and benches (if not set just ask). karla is a great professor and can adapt quickly.

check in is easy just have ID on hand school will give you pin save it for the next time you're there. class is held in the parent center. the students are brought to the class. staff needs to work on establishing expectations a board is available always take advantage of it. small class easy to manage.

check in is easy and so is parking. security lets you in and office manager walks you to the classroom. Michael is a great professor but needs to stop wearing the hat. class went really well he does get a little aggravated in some points but manages to control himself.
